title: auth — validation rules
description: Six validation rules. Five validate opaque tokens against their backing tables (constant-time hash comparison + atomic consume). One composes identity's strong_password with auth-flow-specific augmentation (history check via identity, breach-check via config, plus session-revocation warning surfacing).

rules.name: valid_refresh_token
rules.description: Validates a submitted refresh token against auth_refresh_tokens. Constant-time compares the SHA-256 of the input against every non-consumed, non-revoked, non-expired row's token_hash. On match returns the RefreshToken row for the caller to consume atomically. NEVER consumes on validation — that's the RefreshAction's responsibility.
rules.default_message: The refresh token is invalid, expired, or has already been used.
rules.notes: This rule does NOT flag reuse (already-consumed) — that's a signal the RefreshAction picks up and fires RefreshTokenReuseDetected on. From validation's perspective, an already-consumed token is simply invalid.

rules.name: valid_password_reset_token
rules.description: Validates a submitted reset token against auth_password_resets. Constant-time comparison. On match returns the PasswordReset row.
rules.default_message: The password reset token is invalid, expired, or has already been used.

rules.name: valid_email_verification_token
rules.description: Validates a submitted verification token against auth_email_verifications. Constant-time comparison. On match returns the EmailVerification row. Idempotent — already-consumed tokens for an already-verified identity return the identity's `already_verified` signal so the caller can treat it as success.
rules.default_message: The email verification token is invalid, expired, or has already been used.

rules.name: valid_mfa_challenge_id
rules.description: Validates a submitted challenge_id against auth_mfa_challenges. Refuses when the row is missing, expired, consumed, or has hit auth.mfa.max_attempts_per_challenge.
rules.default_message: The MFA challenge is invalid, expired, or has exceeded max attempts.

rules.name: valid_cross_app_grant_token
rules.description: Validates a submitted grant token against auth_cross_app_grants. Constant-time comparison. On match returns the CrossAppGrant row (still un-consumed). Refuses if the current request's Application ID doesn't match the grant's target_application_id.
rules.default_message: The cross-application grant token is invalid, expired, or already used.

rules.name: strong_password_via_auth
rules.description: Composes identity::strong_password + identity::not_in_password_history against a specific identity_id. Additionally checks auth.password.rehash_on_verify (soft warning surface) and any auth-flow-specific augmentation (currently just history+breach-check delegation).

params.name: identity_id
params.description: The Identity whose history should be checked. Passed from the request context (never trusted from user input).

rules.default_message: Password does not meet security requirements. :reason

usage_from_data_class: Data classes composed with `LoginInput`, `ResetPasswordInput`, `RefreshInput`, `MfaVerifyInput`, `CrossAppExchangeInput` wire the rules via #[Validation] attribute on each property; consumers rarely reference the rules by name.
